segments.title: French Navy fights on in WWII force value
segments.summary: A Patreon live frames a French Navy that keeps fighting in WWII as a major logistics, basing, and fleet-strength addition to Allied planning.
segments.body: The discussion captures the stream's setup for the alternate-history scenario. Dr. Clarke emphasizes that the French Navy would bring worldwide bases, logistics, and infrastructure as well as fleet units, and he treats the fall or survival of those assets as a low-probability but high-risk planning issue for Britain. The later fleet listing shows why the scenario is not just political: the available force includes fast battleships, cruisers, large destroyers, submarines, and other assets that would have to be reckoned with.

evidence.note: The opening frame describes what the French Navy brings, including logistics and its position among the major navies.
The transcript highlights French basing, overseas facilities, North African positions, Suez, and British planning dependence on French assets.
The discussion lists the scale of the French force, including Dunkerque-class fast battleships, battleships, cruisers, large destroyers, torpilleurs, submarines, and a seaplane carrier.
The Brest force example combines Dunkerque and Strasbourg, Bearn, light cruisers, and contre-torpilleurs as a formation requiring serious opposition.
